title: Leo arrives—a little early, a lot loved
body: Leo John Paul Fraser was born at 1:46pm Georgetown time, premature and brave, into the arms of Luke & Dana. The whole family, from the Caymans to New Zealand, started cheering (and sobbing) at once.

title: Settling in & a few firsts
body: Leo is doing OK and keeping the nurses busy—has done some poos. Good boy.

title: Breathing easier & his very first meal
body: Leo is breathing much better today and had his very first feed—1ml of colostrum. Basically a big mac.

title: Into the incubator—and first cuddles
body: A big day: Leo graduated to the incubator, his breathing support is being slowly reduced, and he's now feeding every 2 hours in bigger amounts. Best of all—he got to have his first cuddles with mum & dad. 😭

title: Going from strength to strength
body: Doing well again today and eating more and more—like the little Fraser legend that he is.

title: Off breathing support, all on his own
body: Leo is now off breathing support completely and taking more milk with every feed. He's got a touch of jaundice, so is chilling in the sunbed to even out his tan. Getting that Cayman glow ready.

title: Making his mark on the world
body: Leo has gone from ‘done some poos’ to ‘projectile poos out the incubator door’. A fine young man of pace and dexterity.

title: Graduated to a cot!
body: Leo is moving up in the world—graduating to his own big boy bed.

title: “We made a cute baby, if I do say so myself”—Dad
body: Dad is bias (lawyer after all). But even so, we couldn’t agree more. ❤️
